nodes.title: Recover from suspected session theft
nodes.statement: As an account owner, I want to revoke other active sessions so that I can recover from suspected session theft.

nodes.title: Current session remains active
nodes.statement: Revoking other sessions must not revoke the session authorizing the operation.

nodes.title: Revoke other sessions
nodes.statement: When an account owner requests revocation of all other sessions, the authentication service MUST invalidate every session belonging to the account except the session authorizing the operation.

nodes.title: Revocation latency
nodes.statement: Revocation of all other sessions MUST complete within 2 seconds for an account with up to 50 active sessions.

nodes.title: Other sessions are revoked
nodes.statement: Given an account owner has multiple active sessions, when they revoke all other sessions, then every other session is invalidated and the current session remains active.

nodes.title: Revocation is timely
nodes.statement: Given an account with 50 active sessions, when revocation runs, then it completes within 2 seconds.

nodes.title: Concurrent revocation request
nodes.statement: A second revocation request that arrives while the first is in progress MUST be idempotent and MUST NOT revoke the authorizing session.

nodes.title: Sessions are centrally stored
impact_if_false: FR-1 must be re-evaluated against per-node session storage
